_meta.description: Fix catalogue for the PPD fix list generator. Used by fix-generator.js — bottom dimensions by score (ASC), tiebreak by dimension_id, mapped to a catalogue fix, then top 3 sorted by revenue impact (DESC).

fixes.plain_english_description: The two segments live in Engagement today are single-condition price thresholds (purchase.total_price > $100 / $150) and neither is exposed to Discovery merchandising. Rebuild them with multi-condition filters that combine behaviour, recency, and value, then expose them to Discovery so boost rules can target them.
steps: Open the two existing segmentations in Engagement
Add a 30-day recency window and a behavioural event (view_item / cart_update) to each segment
Layer at least 3 conditions per segment (purchase value + behaviour + recency)
Toggle Discovery integration → exposed for all 3 segments
Confirm each segment shows state "used" in the Discovery integration panel
fixes.impact_assumption: Single-condition price segments target a narrow cohort; multi-condition behavioural+recency segments typically lift personalised search RPV by 10–16%.

fixes.fix_title: Instrument search, wishlist, and survey events
fixes.plain_english_description: The 90d-active user only triggers ~4 distinct event types on average — mostly session_start, session_end, page_visit, and view_item. Rich behavioural signals like search, wishlist_add, cart_update breakdowns, and survey responses are sparsely captured. Instrumenting these events broadens the personalisation signal base for every active customer.
fixes.estimated_revenue_impact: 8–12% RPV lift via richer personalisation signals
steps: Audit the SDK config and confirm session_start, session_end, page_visit are firing
Add the search event with query + result_count properties on every PLP query
Add wishlist_add and wishlist_remove on heart-icon actions
Trigger a survey event from post-purchase NPS responses
Validate in Engagement → Customers → Events that avg distinct types per 90d-active user clears 7
fixes.impact_assumption: Bloomreach benchmarks show that customers with ≥7 distinct event types feed 1.4× richer personalisation models, lifting RPV by 8–12% on personalised surfaces.

fixes.fix_title: Capture email and enrich anonymous profiles
fixes.plain_english_description: Only 20.4% of customers (25,187 of 123,164) have an email populated; the rest are anonymous cookies. Email and basic profile attributes unlock cross-device matching and lifecycle personalisation. Email-capture banners, checkout email-required, and post-purchase profile prompts typically lift identified ratio from ~20% to ~60%.
steps: Add a newsletter / 10%-off email-capture banner site-wide
Make email a required field at checkout (already standard practice)
Add a post-purchase profile-completion prompt (first name, gender, language)
Validate weekly in Engagement: % customers with email populated
fixes.impact_assumption: Lifting identified profile ratio from 20% → 60% restores ~3× the addressable cohort for lifecycle and 1:1 personalisation, typically 6–10% RPV impact.

fixes.fix_title: Enable BRUID persistence for guest sessions
fixes.plain_english_description: Only 22% of site sessions are recognised by Bloomreach's BRUID cookie. The primary cause is that most visitors are not logged in when they arrive — they log in at checkout. Until BRUID persistence is implemented for anonymous sessions, Loomi cannot personalise for 78% of traffic.
steps: Verify _br_uid_2 cookie lifetime is set to 365 days in Discovery configuration
Confirm cookie is not blocked by consent management platform for returning visitors
Test BRUID persistence across sessions in staging environment
Monitor BRUID match rate in Discovery analytics weekly
fixes.impact_assumption: Based on Bloomreach benchmarks: 1 percentage point improvement in BRUID match rate ≈ 0.5–0.8% RPV lift. Moving from 22% → 70% match rate = ~24–38% improvement = estimated 8–15% RPV on returning visitor cohort.

fixes.plain_english_description: Three pre-configured boost rules exist in Discovery but are currently INACTIVE. Activating them will immediately surface the right products for each customer segment — gift products for Gifting Intent, premium and new arrivals for High Value Returning, and bestsellers for New Prospecting visitors.
steps: In Bloomreach Discovery merchandising UI, locate the three pre-created boost rules
Activate Rule 1: Gifting Intent — boost gift_eligible products
Activate Rule 2: High Value Returning — boost is_new_arrival and premium products
Activate Rule 3: New Prospecting — boost is_bestseller products
Monitor search result rankings for each persona in PLP
fixes.impact_assumption: Segment-scoped boost rules with confirmed segment coverage (post-fix 68%) typically drive 5–10% RPV improvement on affected search queries.

fixes.plain_english_description: Three key customer groups — Gifting Intent, High Value Returning, and New Prospecting — are not yet defined as segments in Bloomreach Engagement. Without segments, Loomi cannot apply any personalised ranking rules. Creating these three segments is the single highest-impact action available.
steps: In Bloomreach Engagement, navigate to Segments
Create segment: Gifting Intent (4+ gift category page views OR gift product clicks in last 30 days)
Create segment: High Value Returning (2+ purchases AND AOV > $150)
Create segment: New Prospecting (0 purchases AND session_count = 1)
Link each segment to corresponding Discovery boost rule
fixes.impact_assumption: Based on Bloomreach benchmark data: moving from 0% → 68% AutoSegment coverage with active rules typically drives 12–18% RPV on targeted visitor cohort.
